author: @1337m4n
text:
I never saw much point in remaking games that still hold up perfectly well today. To me, the ideal candidate for a remake is a game with lots of technical problems that make it unpalatable in the present day, but that had excellent concepts, characters, lore, etc.
Take Fallout 1. Great atmosphere, creative worldbuilding that makes you want to learn more, lovable characters. But when it comes to the actual gameplay it's a mess. Half the skills and perks are useless, combat is more RNG than strategy, several quests were cut due to time/budget constraints. This is prime remake material. The flaws are easily fixed and you don't have to sacrifice the strengths to fix them. You could even add in some more content while you're at it (because let's be honest, if it weren't for the sluggish turn-based combat slowing things down, the game would be short as hell).
Unfortunately, the license is currently owned by Bethseda, and if they attempted a remake they would almost surely find some way to fuck it up.

author: @ashurasama7510
text:
billy hatcher and the giant egg....I feel so old. and depressed.
Me and my sis played that game to death as kids, and it was amazing. I really loved the soundtrack(always was stuck in my head, even now), level designs and character, billy hatcher. and we kept trying to see if we could unlock sonic in this game haha. But out of the blue, we ended up loosing the game disc. I remember me and my sis flipping the house upside down(while our mom yelled at us) trying to find the CD.i could only believe it was either our baby sister, or our cousins that came over frequently and probably stole it. It was never found. and even to this day, I still have hope that its still somewhere in the house...but even if it was, the disc wouldt probably be no good anymore.
 Rip.

author: @Unithrex
text:
Hey Dunkey, I know this is an old video but there is a mod for the PC emulator that lets you use analog controls for Mario 64 DS. There's also a version for the Wii U's VC release and even one for when you're playing it on 3DS. 

I'm playing it on 3DS right now with a widescreen mod and it is more of a remix of Mario 64 rather than a straight up remake / remaster. Still a great time with lots of new mechanics and levels and the different characters play really different too. It's really fun way to revisit Mario 64!

author: @givemeyamilk1938
text: So, I recently bought the HD collection of MGS. It has 3,2, PW, and the first and second Metal Gears. The graphics are exactly the same as the ps2 games, but I think it doesn’t matter, because it allowed me to play some of the best Metal Gear games. If it allows you to experience something from a couple years before for the first time like people did then, I think it can be excused. That’s a rare exception though, because it is like you said, some of it is just lazy cash grabs. I only say it because you could potentially get a game that is extremely rare for a couple bucks, instead of a fortune, not including the system required to play it. There is just too small a chance you’re gonna find games like Snake Eater or RE2 for cheap, and in good condition.

author: @CarterAvalancheAllen
text:
4:10 Modern Warfare actually did a ton of good things for the remaster.

- They added being able to see how many Intel files are in each level and how many you’d collected so you don’t have to backtrack to every level and guess which ones you missed or got.

- added the ability to go to your last checkpoint without saving and quitting every time

- there is also a lower difficulty button much like MW2/3 

- Added an unused training mode to teach grenades

- They added sensitivity for camera movement so you can turn your head faster

-They also added trophies for PS because the original PS3 version didn’t.

- New achievements such as beating the developer’s FNG Ship record, destroying a helicopter in the stealth mission, do a level without reloading, only using knife, land 5 headshots with a desert eagle, use solely the javelin on the tanks, etc.)

author: @Jebronjarden
text: The return to arkham remaster Is the perfect example of the art direction ruining the remaster. The textures are much nicer but because they changed up some character models, put a new green filter on the game compared to the more blue, gray filter of the older games and changed up the lighting, the game looks worse. I encourage anyone to look up side by side comparisons of the remaster vs the original arkham games. You're gonna see how the downgrade in lighting and the weird filter makes the game look worse than the original despite the better textures.
